statement: Most new product features fail to improve the metrics they were designed to improve.
evidence_basis: A/B testing data from IMVU; industry benchmarks
falsifiability: Disproved if >50% of tested features succeed
business_implication: Default assumption for any feature should be failure; prioritize validation loops over waterfall dev

statement: AI-assisted planning cuts route-mapping time for extraterrestrial rovers by 50% while maintaining safety.
evidence_basis: NASA operational data from Claude-planned Mars drives (Dec 2025)
falsifiability: Disproved if manual planning proves faster or safer in subsequent trials
business_implication: Remote autonomy is now viable for high-latency environments (mining, space, deep sea); shift from tele-operation to goal-setting.

statement: AI-powered screening detects 27% more aggressive tumors and cuts expert workload by 44%.
evidence_basis: Two-year study of 100,000+ women; published research
falsifiability: Disproved by larger multi-center randomized control trials showing null effect
business_implication: Diagnostic labor costs can be halved while improving outcomes; distinct arbitrage opportunity in insurtech/care delivery.

statement: Small, open-source/efficient models now outperform frontier models on elite benchmarks; Deepseek v4 confirmed the 'Efficiency > Scale' thesis.
source: The Rundown (Nous Research) / Deepseek v4 Launch (Feb 2026)
evidence_basis: Nomos-1 scored 87/120 on Putnam, beating Qwen-3 (24/120). Deepseek v4 matched GPT-5 at 1/10th the compute cost, proving open-weights efficiency is structural, not anomalous.
falsifiability: Disproved if results are not reproducible or are result of data contamination.
business_implication: The 'Moat' of massive parameter count is collapsing; specialized reasoning models <100B params are the future of vertical AI.

statement: Mobile AI usage is dominated by health/wellness queries, functioning as a round-the-clock companion.
evidence_basis: Analysis of 37.5M Copilot conversations; distinct mobile vs desktop patterns.
falsifiability: Disproved if usage data shifts significantly in subsequent quarters.
business_implication: Mobile AI interfaces should pivot from 'Chat' to 'Care/Coaching' metaphors; value is in intimacy/health tracking, not coding/productivity.

statement: IP licensing is the new frontier for generative video; unauthorized generation faces imminent legal shutdowns.
evidence_basis: Disney's $1B stake in OpenAI + Cease-and-Desist to Google.
falsifiability: Disproved if courts rule fair use for IP in generative models (unlikely given trend).
business_implication: Startups relying on 'wild web' scraping for video/image gen are dead; the only viable path is licensed data moats (Getty, Disney, etc.).

statement: Open-source AI development is shifting from 'open weights' to 'open agents' and 'open workflows', bypassing model training costs.
evidence_basis: Rise of tools like Orkes Conductor and open-source agent recipes (AI2) outpacing pure model releases.
falsifiability: Disproved if open-source community reverts to focusing primarily on parameter scaling wars.
business_implication: Value capture moves from the 'Model Layer' to the 'Orchestration Layer'; build tools for connecting agents, not training them.

statement: Sleep data can predict 130+ distinct disease categories (including dementia and heart attacks) with >80% accuracy.
evidence_basis: Stanford study on 600K hours of sleep data from 65K participants.
falsifiability: Disproved if predictions fail validation in broader, diverse clinical populations.
business_implication: Sleep trackers (Whoop/Oura/Apple Watch) are no longer wellness toys but FDA-grade diagnostic devices; insurance arbitrage opportunity for preventative sleep monitoring.

statement: Autonomous prescription renewal by AI is now legally viable in US states.
evidence_basis: Utah state law change approving AI for 191 drug classes; Doctronic implementation.
falsifiability: Disproved if legislation is repealed or key pilots result in patient harm/lawsuits.
business_implication: Regulatory moats in healthcare are crumbling faster than expected; 'AI-as-a-Prescriber' business models are now investable.

statement: Context switching is the primary bottleneck for AI Agent reliability, not model intelligence.
evidence_basis: Enterprise failure rates due to 'stateless' agent execution; success of 'Context Graph' products (PlayerZero).
falsifiability: Disproved if model scaling (GPT-6) solves reliability without external context management.
business_implication: Don't build 'smarter' agents; build 'state machines' (Detailed workflows/memory) that keep dumb agents on track.

statement: AI 'Commerce Protocols' are replacing direct-to-consumer websites.
evidence_basis: Google + Shopify/Walmart launch of UCP (Universal Commerce Protocol) for agentic shopping.
falsifiability: Disproved if consumers reject agent-mediated shopping and return to browsing storefronts.
business_implication: SEO is dead; 'AEO' (Agent Engine Optimization) is the new game. Brands must optimize data for *agents* to read, not humans.

statement: Heavy reliance on AI coding assistants reduces mastery and retention in junior engineers by ~17%.
evidence_basis: Randomized controlled trial of 52 engineers; AI users scored lower on post-task debugging quizzes.
falsifiability: Disproved if long-term studies show that AI-assisted learners catch up or surpass manual learners.
business_implication: Companies need 'Manual Mode' training weeks to prevent 'Skill Rot'. A market exists for 'AI-Free' certification or rigorous technical assessments.

statement: Interactive World Generation (Text-to-World) has achieved technical viability, allowing real-time navigation of generated spaces.
evidence_basis: Public release of Genie 3; users exploring generated worlds in real-time (60s limit).
falsifiability: Disproved if the tech hits a compute wall and cannot scale beyond short, low-res clips.
business_implication: The gaming/sim pipeline will shift from 'Asset creation' (geometry/textures) to 'Latent Space Curation'. Engines like Unreal will integrate generative world models.

statement: The 'Content' Asset Class has been destroyed from both sides: Supply-side (AI flooding infinite commodity content) AND Demand-side (AI Answers replacing search traffic/links). Content sites are uninvestable. [MERGED from T-037]
source: Inversion of 'The ChatGPT Millionaire' + Stratechery (Ben Thompson)
evidence_basis: Supply: The ChatGPT Millionaire teaches flooding the market with unlimited free content. Demand: Google SGE/Perplexity destroyed referral traffic to niche content sites (Aggregation Theory 2.0). Both sides confirm content value = zero.
falsifiability: Disproved if the price per word of generic SEO copywriting increases in 2026 OR if zero-click searches decrease and traffic flows back to publishers.
business_implication: Stop selling 'Information'. The only value is being a 'Primary Source' that AI is forced to cite, or pivoting to 'Verification', 'Tools', or 'Physical Goods'.

statement: 'Synthetic Expertise' is a bubble; The market will violently correct against 'Act as a Nutritionist' advice.
evidence_basis: The book advises using AI to simulate expertise in law, medicine, and nutrition without training. This creates a 'Lemons Market' where trust in *all* online advice collapses.
falsifiability: Disproved if AI-generated medical advice becomes the legal and cultural standard without human oversight.
business_implication: Build 'Trust Infrastructure'. High-Verification Brands (e.g., 'Verified Human', 'Clinically Backed') will command a massive premium over the 'Slop'.

statement: 'Agent Engine Optimization' (AEO) is the primary growth driver for e-commerce; Human SEO is vanity.
evidence_basis: Triple Whale merchants saw LLM-referred orders explode from 7,152 (2024) to 424,000+ (Q4 2025).
falsifiability: Disproved if LLM referral traffic plateaus or is blocked by platforms.
business_implication: Measuring 'Human Traffic' is a vanity metric. If your site isn't optimized for `Agent.json` parsing, you are invisible to the actual buyers.

statement: A 'Genius' model is less effective than a 'Team' of mid-level models; Scaling is Horizontal (Swarms), Not Vertical (Context).
evidence_basis: Anthropic explicitly pivoted to 'Agent Teams'—splitting tasks among specialized instances—rather than just relying on their massive 1M context window.
falsifiability: Disproved if a single, massive context model consistently outperforms a swarm on complex benchmarks (SWE-Bench).
business_implication: The future of dev tools is 'Multi-Agent Orchestration' (managing hand-offs), not just Prompt Engineering (managing one output).

statement: Agent 'Depth' (Time Horizon) is the new competitive vector; simply adding more agents (Width) hits diminishing returns.
evidence_basis: Opus 4.6 and Codex 5.3 now target multi-week task coherence, proving that 'More Agents' (Swarms) failed to solve complex logic without deep context.
falsifiability: Disproved if massive parallel swarms of small models outperform deep-context single models on long tasks.
business_implication: Pivot from building 'Swarms' (Orchestration) to building 'Deep State' (Memory/Context Persistence) for single agents.

statement: Short-Form Video (TikTok) contains 50x higher 'Action Density' per minute than long-form.
evidence_basis: ByteDance's new model demonstrates superior physics reasoning because it trains on high-entropy clips (dense motion) rather than low-entropy movies (talking heads).
falsifiability: Disproved if YouTube-trained models consistently beat TikTok-trained models on physics benchmarks.
business_implication: Train Generative Video models on 'Reels/Shorts' (Action), not 'Cinema' (Narrative).

statement: The drone delivery market will be bottlenecked not by technology, but by airspace regulation; the FAA's BVLOS rules are the only moat.
source: FAA Part 107 / Wing & Zipline Operational Data
evidence_basis: Wing (Alphabet) and Zipline logged 1M+ commercial deliveries but only in FAA-exempt zones. No company has scaled beyond regulatory waivers.
falsifiability: Disproved if FAA grants blanket BVLOS authorization and multiple companies scale simultaneously.
business_implication: Don't build better drones; build better regulatory relationships. The moat is legal, not technical.

statement: Vertical farming (hydroponics/aeroponics) unit economics only work for high-value leafy greens; the 'Feed the World' narrative is false.
evidence_basis: AppHarvest bankruptcy (2023), AeroFarms Chapter 11 (2023). Unit economics negative for commodity crops (corn, wheat). Only lettuce/herbs/microgreens ROI-positive at current energy costs.
falsifiability: Disproved if a vertical farming company profitably grows staple crops (corn, rice, wheat) at scale.
business_implication: Vertical farming is a luxury produce play, not a food security play. Invest in high-margin botanicals, not commodity calories.

statement: Premium domain names follow Lindy Effect dynamics; a domain's value increases with age, unlike any other digital asset.
source: NameBio Transaction Data / Verisign Registry Reports
evidence_basis: 20-year-old one-word .coms sell for $1M-$50M+ (Voice.com, Sex.com). New TLDs (.xyz, .io) depreciate. Registrars confirm negative churn on aged premium inventory.
falsifiability: Disproved if new TLD domains (.ai, .xyz) consistently outsell aged .com equivalents at auction.
business_implication: Domain names are the only digital asset with positive aging dynamics. A portfolio of aged .coms is a superior store of value to NFTs.

statement: XR adoption follows the 'Eyewear Curve' — mass adoption requires sub-100g devices that look like normal glasses, not ski goggles.
source: Apple Vision Pro Sales Data / Meta Ray-Ban Market Analysis
evidence_basis: Apple Vision Pro sales collapsed Q2 2025 (500g). Meta Ray-Ban Stories (under 50g) outsold it 10:1. Snap Spectacles confirmed sub-100g is the adoption threshold.
falsifiability: Disproved if a heavy (>200g) XR headset achieves >50M unit sales in a single year.
business_implication: Don't invest in 'computing goggles'. Invest in miniaturization companies (micro-OLED, waveguide optics) that enable the form factor shift.

statement: Prediction markets are more accurate than polls, pundits, and AI models for binary event forecasting.
evidence_basis: Polymarket called 2024 US election 48 hours before any poll. Kalshi outperformed FiveThirtyEight on 78% of resolvable contracts. Skin-in-the-game eliminates social desirability bias.
falsifiability: Disproved if polls or AI forecast models systematically outperform prediction markets on >100 binary events.
business_implication: Prediction markets are the most efficient information aggregation mechanism ever built. Any decision-making process not incorporating market prices is leaving accuracy on the table.

statement: SCOTUS ruled 6-3 (Learning Resources v. Trump, Feb 20, 2026) that IEEPA does not authorize tariffs, stripping the Executive of its primary unilateral trade weapon and creating a $175B refund liability.
evidence_basis: 6-3 decision authored by Roberts. IEEPA's 'regulate importation' language does not extend to levying tariffs. $175B in duties collected under IEEPA are legally owed back. Trump immediately pivoted to Section 122 (Trade Act 1974) as a 150-day stopgap.
falsifiability: Disproved if Congress passes legislation retroactively validating IEEPA tariffs or if SCOTUS reverses on rehearing (extremely unlikely given 6-3 margin).
business_implication: Executive tariff authority is structurally weakened. Any business model dependent on tariff stability must now model a 'Tariff Vacuum' between July 2026 (Section 122 expiry) and whenever Section 232/301 investigations can legally conclude. Importers who paid IEEPA duties should file CBP protests immediately under 19 USC §1514.

statement: Bitcoin's holder base has structurally shifted from HODLers to institutional risk-parity funds via $50B+ in ETF AUM, making historical seasonality and 'Fear & Greed' recovery patterns unreliable.
source: BlackRock IBIT / Fidelity FBTC Flow Data / CryptoQuant Exchange Whale Ratio
evidence_basis: 5 consecutive weeks of ETF outflows ($3.8B cumulative, Feb 2026). Exchange whale ratio at highest since 2015. $450M in leveraged liquidations in a single 24-hour period. BTC price dropped from $126K ATH to ~$64.8K (-49%) while institutional outflows accelerated. This pattern matches TradFi de-risking behavior, not crypto-native capitulation.
falsifiability: Disproved if BTC demonstrates a V-shaped recovery from $60-65K levels despite continued ETF outflows, proving the retail/HODLer bid is still dominant.
business_implication: BTC is now a correlated TradFi asset, not a 'digital gold' safe haven. Historical crypto seasonality data (e.g., 'February is bullish, 14.3% avg return') is meaningless when the marginal buyer is a risk-parity fund, not a retail HODLer. Portfolio construction must treat BTC exposure as equity-correlated beta, not uncorrelated alpha.

statement: Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 has a hard 150-day expiry clause that automatically kills the tariff without Congressional action; Congress has historically never voted to extend Section 122 tariffs.
source: Trade Act of 1974 / CFR Legal Analysis / Congressional Research Service
evidence_basis: Trump invoked Section 122 on Feb 20, 2026, imposing a 10% (later 15%) global tariff. The statute limits these tariffs to 150 days (~July 20, 2026) without explicit Congressional authorization for extension. Both the House and Senate previously voted to disapprove IEEPA tariffs. Three GOP defectors would kill any extension vote given the thin majority.
falsifiability: Disproved if Congress passes a bipartisan Section 122 extension bill before July 2026.
business_implication: A hard, legally mandated 'Tariff Cliff' exists on or around July 20, 2026. Businesses should plan for a tariff-free gap between this date and whenever Section 232/301 tariffs can be legally imposed (6-12 months of investigations). Container spot rates, bonded warehouse utilization, and lobbying disclosure data are the leading indicators of whether the market has priced this cliff.

statement: Agentic AI tools expand, rather than lighten, employee workloads; unfamiliar work becomes doable, blurring role boundaries and creating 'vibe-coding' QA pileups.
evidence_basis: 8-month study of ~200 tech sector employees showing expanded roles, more multitasking, and longer logged hours despite using AI.
falsifiability: Disproved if a multi-year study shows aggregate reduction in working hours or narrower job scopes post-AI adoption.
business_implication: Do not sell AI as a 'time-saver' to employees; sell it as a 'capability multiplier' to management. Prepare for structural shifts in job descriptions and burnout from after-hours prompting.

statement: The distance between a model's raw intelligence (benchmarks) and its actual utility is widening due to the 'tools gap.'
evidence_basis: Gemini 3.1 Pro dominates benchmarks (ARC-AGI-2 at 77.1%) but loses to Claude Opus 4.6 on actual knowledge work (GDPval-AA) because Claude has superior tool integrations (Cowork, Excel, PPT).
falsifiability: Disproved if a model with inferior tool integrations beats a highly integrated model purely through raw zero-shot reasoning in real-world workflows.
business_implication: Model benchmarks are vanity metrics for end-users. Value capture is entirely in the orchestration and tool layer (the 'Agent OS'). Build wrappers that execute actions, not wrappers that just chat.

statement: The next leap in computer-use agents is driven by learning from video (FDM-1), bypassing the limitations of text-only training data.
evidence_basis: FDM-1 trained on 11M hours of screen footage, proving capable of finding bugs, doing CAD modeling, and driving a car.
falsifiability: Disproved if text-only models consistently beat video-trained models on GUI navigation and continuous spatial-temporal tasks.
business_implication: The most valuable training data is no longer text—it is screen recordings of professionals working. Companies should aggressively log internal video/screen data for future foundational model training or licensing.

statement: Advanced AI development is silently relying on systemic model distillation (theft) from frontier models like Claude to map capability gains.
evidence_basis: DeepSeek, MiniMax, and Moonshot ran 16M+ fraudulent exchanges to siphon Claude's logic and censorship behavior to train their own models.
falsifiability: Disproved if Chinese models maintain state-of-the-art equivalence after Anthropic successfully blocks all automated scraping vectors.
business_implication: The 'Moat' of AI intelligence is porous. Closed models are inevitably distilled into open-source or competitor equivalents within months. Focus monetization on proprietary actions and private data integrations, not raw reasoning.

statement: Extreme inference speed requires permanently baking a single model directly onto custom silicon instead of running it as software.
evidence_basis: HC1 chip embedded with Llama 3.1 8B produces responses 100x faster (under 100ms) than standard hardware by removing general-purpose overhead.
falsifiability: Disproved if general-purpose GPUs like Nvidia H100s can match the sub-100ms latency of hardcoded chips for the same energy cost.
business_implication: The future of edge AI and agentic 'fast-thinking' (e.g. self-driving, robotics, voice) relies on hardcoded application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs) for specific models, creating a massive new semiconductor niche.
